text: hey, I just set up the new React Native 0.73 project scaffold with Hermes engine enabled by default. Can you take a look at the metro bundler config? I want to make sure tree shaking is working properly before we start adding screens

nice, pulling it now. I'll run a baseline bundle size report before we add anything so we have a reference point. Last time Hermes bytecode compilation shaved off like 30% on Android — want to verify that holds

Henry, quick sync — I've drafted the activation metrics we want to track for the mobile app launch. Key ones are: time-to-first-action, push notification opt-in rate, and day-7 retention. Can we chat at 2pm about what's feasible to instrument in v1?
2pm works. I think we can get time-to-first-action and push opt-in for v1 easily — day-7 retention will need @ryan_nguyen to set up the analytics events. Let me loop him in

@ryan_nguyen we're defining activation metrics for the mobile app — Priya wants time-to-first-action, push notification opt-in rate, and day-7 retention. Can you spec out the analytics events we'd need? We're using React Native so everything goes through the same JS bridge

starting on the mobile app event taxonomy. For push notification tracking do we want to differentiate between iOS and Android opt-in flows? They're pretty different permission-wise. @priya_mehta @henry_zhao thoughts?
yeah definitely — iOS has the permission prompt that only fires once, Android is auto-granted until API 33. We should track them separately in the analytics dashboard

baseline bundle size report for the mobile app is done. iOS: 4.2MB, Android: 3.8MB with Hermes engine. That's solid. I'll set up a CI check to flag any PR that increases bundle size by more than 100kb

FYI — the mobile-app repo is up and running. React Native 0.73 with Hermes engine, new architecture enabled. If anyone needs to test shared components on mobile, ping me and I'll get you set up with the iOS simulator or Android emulator
hey Henry, I'm working on the shared Button and TextInput components. Do you want me to make them work with React Native out of the box or should I create separate native variants?

separate native variants would be better — React Native's StyleSheet API is different enough from CSS that trying to share styles gets messy fast. But we can share the prop interfaces and validation logic

I'm starting on React Native variants of our shared components for the mobile app. Creating a packages/mobile-ui directory — will have Button, TextInput, and Card ported by end of week. Platform-specific styles with shared prop interfaces

Mobile app activation metrics doc is live — linking in the thread. Key north star: 40% of new installs complete first action within 24 hours. @ryan_nguyen is building the analytics dashboard for this. Launch target is end of March

event taxonomy doc is done. I've defined 14 events covering the full activation funnel — from app_open through push_notification_opted_in to first_action_completed. Want to review before I share with eng?
yes please send it over, I'll review this afternoon. Make sure we have deep link attribution events too — we want to track which deep links drive the most activations
I'm setting up push notifications with Firebase Cloud Messaging for Android and APNs for iOS. The React Native firebase package is pretty heavy — any way we can lazy-load it to keep launch time down?

yeah we can use inline requires in the metro bundler config to defer loading the firebase module until the user actually triggers a notification flow. I'll set that up. Should save ~200ms on cold launch time

heads up — we need a backend endpoint for registering push notification device tokens. The mobile app will send the token on login + whenever it refreshes. @charlie_ross can your team add a POST /devices/register endpoint?

PSA: if you're adding dependencies to the mobile-app, please check the bundle size impact first. Run `npx react-native-bundle-visualizer` and screenshot the diff. We're trying to keep the Android APK under 15MB for emerging markets

hey Grace, the Button component looks great on iOS but the touch feedback is off on Android — React Native's Pressable has different ripple behavior. Can you add a platform-specific android_ripple prop?
oh good catch. I'll use Platform.select to add the ripple config on Android and the opacity feedback on iOS. Will push the fix today
Henry, what's our TestFlight timeline looking like? I need to get the internal beta out to stakeholders by Feb 21 so they can test before the all-hands
doable. The core navigation and auth flow are working. Push notifications are in progress. I'll cut a TestFlight build by end of next week — Feb 14 at the latest. That gives us a week buffer

I ran performance profiling on the main screen and the FlatList is janky on Android — dropping to 45fps when scrolling fast. The Hermes engine's GC is kicking in too often. I'm going to try switching to FlashList and reducing re-renders

tip for anyone working on React Native lists: FlashList recycles views instead of unmounting like FlatList does. Huge perf win. Also you can tune Hermes GC settings in the metro bundler config — there's a heapSize option that helps with janky scroll on Android

update on mobile-app performance: switched the feed list from FlatList to FlashList and frame rate went from 45fps to 58fps on a mid-range Android device. Also tuned Hermes GC — launch time improved by 150ms. Bundle size went up 40kb which is within budget

analytics SDK is integrated. I've wired up the core events — app_open, screen_view, push_notification_opted_in, and first_action_completed. Testing on the iOS simulator now. Can you verify the Android events are firing too?
will check tonight. Make sure the deep link events include the utm_source parameter — we need that for the activation metrics attribution

first TestFlight build for the mobile app is uploading now. Version 0.1.0, build 1. Had to fix some code signing issues with the provisioning profile but it's compiling clean. Should be available in TestFlight in about 30 min
nice! what's the IPA size?
22MB before App Store thinning. Should come down to ~14MB on device. Android APK is 12.5MB which is right where we want it

first TestFlight build for the mobile app is live! Check your TestFlight invites. Please test the core flows: login, feed, push notification opt-in. Report any crashes in #frontend. Android APK is linked in the mobile-app channel too

just installed the TestFlight build — the onboarding flow feels smooth! One thing: can we add a skip button for the push notification permission screen? Some users will say no and we don't want them stuck
good call. I'll add a "maybe later" link below the prompt. On iOS we only get one shot at the system permission dialog so we should probably show our own explainer screen first anyway

Card and Avatar components are done for React Native mobile-ui. Also added a Skeleton loader with shimmer animation that matches the web version. PR is up — @henry_zhao can you review when you get a chance?

reviewing now. The Skeleton looks great. One small thing — can you use React Native's Animated API instead of reanimated for the shimmer? We're trying to minimize native module dependencies to keep the bundle size in check
sure, I'll swap it out. The built-in Animated API should be fine for a simple opacity loop
@henry_zhao the bundle size went up 400kb after the deep links PR — looks like the branch library is pulling in a ton of native code. Can we evaluate a lighter alternative or strip unused modules?
ugh. Let me check if we can use react-native-url-polyfill with a custom linking config instead. That's way lighter. The Branch SDK might be overkill for our deep link use case right now
agreed. If we just need universal links on iOS and app links on Android, we can do it with the built-in React Navigation deep linking support. No extra dependency needed

update: reverted the Branch SDK for deep links and set them up natively using React Navigation's built-in linking. Bundle size back down to baseline. iOS universal links working, Android app links working. No third-party dependency needed
activation metrics dashboard is live. Early TestFlight data: 72% of testers complete first action within 10 minutes, push opt-in rate is 58%. Sample size is small (18 testers) but directionally good

58% push opt-in is solid for internal testers. The real number will probably be lower with external users. Can you add a breakdown by platform? I suspect iOS is lower because of the permission prompt friction

added platform split to the mobile app activation metrics dashboard — iOS and Android are now tracked separately. Also wired up deep link attribution so we can see which entry points drive the highest activation rate. Dashboard link in thread

deep links are fully working on both iOS and Android now. Format is bsus://screen/param — e.g. bsus://post/123 opens the post detail screen. React Navigation handles the routing. No third-party SDK needed
mobile app TestFlight demo went great at the all-hands! Stakeholders are excited. We got feedback to add a share sheet for deep link sharing — @henry_zhao can we get that in for the next build?
yeah, React Native's Share API makes that easy. I'll add it to the post detail screen this week. Users can share bsus:// deep links or a web fallback URL

I've been running performance profiling with Flipper and the React DevTools profiler. Found a re-render hotspot in the feed screen — the header component is re-rendering on every scroll event. Should be easy to fix with React.memo
nice find. Yeah wrap it in memo and also check if we're passing inline style objects — those break memoization since they create new references every render

mobile app perf wins: moved all inline styles to StyleSheet.create and memoized the feed header. Feed scroll is butter smooth now — solid 60fps on the Pixel 6a. Launch time is 1.2s on Android with Hermes engine which is well within our target

mobile-app TestFlight build 0.2.0 is out. Changes: deep links, share sheet, performance fixes (60fps feed scroll), push notification pre-permission screen. Please update via TestFlight and report any crashes
the crash rate on the last TestFlight build was 2.3% — mostly on older iPhones. Can we look into that? For App Store approval Apple wants crash rate under 1%

@frank_li Priya flagged a 2.3% crash rate on TestFlight, mostly on older iPhones. Can you pull the crash logs from Crashlytics? I suspect it's a memory issue — the image caching might be too aggressive on devices with 2GB RAM
confirmed — it's OOM crashes on iPhone 8 and SE. The image cache is holding ~150MB in memory. I'll add a memory pressure listener and cap the cache at 50MB on devices with less than 3GB RAM

shipped the OOM fix for older iPhones. Also fixed ANR (Application Not Responding) events on Android settings screen — we were doing a synchronous AsyncStorage read on the main thread. Moved it to useEffect. Crash rate should drop significantly on the next TestFlight build
great catches. Let's keep an eye on crash rate and ANR rate over the next few days. If we can get crash rate under 0.5% we'll be in really good shape for the App Store review

just published mobile-ui v0.3.0 to our internal npm registry. Includes Button, TextInput, Card, Avatar, Skeleton, and Badge components — all optimized for React Native with platform-specific feedback. @henry_zhao @frank_li give it a spin
these are looking really polished. The Badge component is exactly what we need for the notification count on the tab bar. Integrating it now

mobile app analytics update: TestFlight build 0.2.0 — 85% first-action completion rate, average launch time 1.3s, push opt-in 61% overall (iOS: 52%, Android: 73%). Deep link activation rate is 2.4x higher than organic — we should invest more in deep link distribution
the deep link activation number is huge. Can you break down which deep link patterns are driving the most conversions? I want to prioritize which screens we build share functionality for

top 3 deep link patterns by activation: 1) post detail (bsus://post/:id) — 38% of deep link entries, 2) profile (bsus://user/:id) — 28%, 3) invite (bsus://invite/:code) — 22%. Invite links have the highest completion rate at 91%

mobile app crash rate is down to 0.4% and ANR rate on Android is 0.1% after @frank_li's fixes. We're ready to start the App Store and Google Play submission. Setting up the store listings and screenshots this week
sweet! Before you submit, let me run one more bundle size audit. I want to make sure we haven't crept up since the last report

Henry, for the App Store listing — I've drafted the description and keywords. Can you review the technical accuracy? I mentioned Hermes-powered performance and instant deep links as selling points. Also @lisa_chen is helping with the screenshots

looks good. I'd remove the Hermes mention from the user-facing copy — users don't care about the JS engine. Keep the performance claims though, they're accurate. Launch time under 1.5s, 60fps scrolling

final bundle size report for mobile app v1.0: iOS 13.8MB on device (after App Store thinning), Android APK 11.2MB. Both well under targets. Metro bundler tree shaking is stripping dead code from unused React Navigation screens perfectly

mobile app v1.0 is submitted to the App Store and Google Play! App Store review usually takes 24-48 hours. Final stats: iOS 13.8MB, Android 11.2MB, launch time <1.5s, crash rate 0.4%, 60fps feed scroll. Huge thanks to @frank_li @grace_park @ryan_nguyen @priya_mehta

huge milestone! The Bsus mobile app has been submitted to both app stores. This has been a 5-week sprint and the team crushed it. Activation metrics are looking incredibly strong from TestFlight data. Stay tuned for launch!
congrats on the submission! Quick q — should I keep maintaining the mobile-ui package separately or should we merge it into the main component library with platform detection?
let's keep it separate for now. The React Native StyleSheet API and web CSS are different enough that a shared package would get messy. We can revisit once React Native's new style system matures
App Store review came back — approved! Google Play is approved too. We can publish whenever we're ready. Do you want to coordinate the launch with marketing?
yes! Let's target Wednesday March 12 for the launch. That gives us 2 days to prep the blog post and email blast. Can you do a final TestFlight build for the release candidate?
the Bsus mobile app is LIVE on the App Store and Google Play! Go download it and leave a review if you can. Deep links work from day one — share any bsus:// link and it'll open right in the app
day 1 numbers: 342 installs, 67% first-action completion rate, 48% push opt-in (iOS: 41%, Android: 58%). Deep link entries are 28% of total installs. Launch time p50 is 1.1s on iOS, 1.4s on Android

67% first-action completion is above our 40% target! The push opt-in on iOS is lower than TestFlight though. Can you check if we're losing people on the pre-permission explainer screen? Maybe we need to tweak the copy

post-launch mobile app performance is stable. Crash rate 0.3% across both platforms, zero ANRs reported. Hermes engine memory usage steady at ~80MB. Metro bundler source maps are huge in debug builds but that doesn't affect production users
solid. Let's set up automated performance regression tests in CI — I want to catch any launch time or bundle size regressions before they hit production. We can use Detox for the E2E side

week 1 mobile app metrics: 1,247 installs, 64% first-action rate (target was 40%!), 4.6 App Store rating from 38 reviews, crash rate 0.3%. Deep link entries have 2.1x higher retention than organic. Planning v1.1 roadmap now — @ryan_nguyen can you pull the feature request data?
pulling it now. Top requests from App Store reviews: offline mode, dark theme, and tablet layout. I'll have the full breakdown with activation impact estimates by tomorrow

kicking off v1.1 planning. Top priorities are offline mode and dark theme. @frank_li offline will need a local SQLite cache — can you size the bundle size impact? @grace_park dark theme will need all the mobile-ui components to support dynamic color tokens. Let's sync Thursday
